steps.title: Use the domain
steps.content: Both functions have base $1.3\gt1$, so they increase as $x$ increases. Since $x\ge0$, each function has its minimum at $x=0$.

steps.title: Check function g
steps.content: For $g(x)=20(1.12)(1.3)^{x+3}$, the minimum is $$g(0)=20(1.12)(1.3)^3$$ This value is not displayed as a single constant or coefficient in the equation for $g$.

steps.title: Check function h
steps.content: For $h(x)=20(1.3)^{x+5}$, the minimum is $$h(0)=20(1.3)^5$$ This value is not displayed as a single constant or coefficient in the equation for $h$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Neither equation displays the minimum value as a constant or coefficient, so D is correct.
